summary.goal: Dynamically calculate default window size from the active theme's monospace font metrics at runtime for optimal 100-120 character readability
summary.description:
The default window size must be dynamically calculated from the active theme's font metrics at runtime.

## Character Width Standards
- Classic terminal: 80 characters
- Modern markdown/docs: 100-120 characters (user confirmed)
- LLM-generated content: typically optimized for ~100-120 chars
- ASCII diagrams: usually designed for 80-100 chars

## Dynamic Calculation (Per-Theme)
- Use NSFont.advancementForGlyph() for exact character width
- Target: 100-120 characters visible width
- Window width = (char_width x target_chars) + content_padding + window_chrome
- Recalculate when theme changes (negligible cost)
- Example at SF Mono 13pt: ~7.8px/char → ~860-1000px window width
- Example at JetBrains Mono 13pt: ~8.0px/char → ~880-1020px window width

## Height
- Proportional to width — roughly 4:3 or golden ratio
- Capped to not exceed 85% of screen height

## Multi-Monitor Handling
- Window centers on the display where the user's mouse cursor is located
- Uses NSScreen.main (screen with key window) for first launch, then cursor-based for subsequent
- Respects display scaling: calculation uses logical points, not physical pixels
- On Retina displays, font metrics and window sizing work in points (Retina-safe)
- If window would exceed display bounds, scale down proportionally while maintaining character count target

## Window Constraints
- **Minimum size**: 60 characters wide (below this, markdown tables break badly)
- **Maximum size**: 90% of screen width (leave some desktop visible)
- **Minimum height**: 400 points (enough for meaningful content)
- **Resizable**: User can resize freely; content reflows. No size memory between launches.

## Font Size Interaction
- When user changes font size (Cmd+Plus/Minus), window does NOT resize
- Content reflows within existing window dimensions
- Character count per line decreases/increases with font size change
- Window size is based on DEFAULT font size, not current user-adjusted size

## Resolved Design Decisions
- Window width calculated dynamically per theme, not hardcoded
- Use point-based sizing (Retina-safe)
- Content padding creates breathing room between window edge and text
- Window does not resize on font size change — content reflows
- Line height differences between fonts: include in height calculation but not width

acceptance_criteria.description: Window width calculated from theme's monospace font metrics at runtime
Window size recalculates when theme changes
New windows center on display where user's cursor is located

observations: NSFont.advancementForGlyph() for exact character width measurement
Window width = (char_width x target_chars) + padding + chrome
Height proportional to width (4:3 or golden ratio), capped at 85% screen
NSScreen.main for display detection, cursor-based for subsequent windows
100-120 character target width for modern markdown readability
Font size change reflows content, doesn't resize window

steps.title: Implement font metrics measurement for character width
steps.description: Create a FontMetrics utility that measures the exact character width for any monospace font at a given size. Use NSFont.advancementForGlyph() with a representative glyph (e.g., 'M' or '0' — all glyphs should be the same width in monospace). Cache the result per font+size combination since it is used frequently. Verify measurements are in logical points (not pixels) for Retina safety.

acceptance_criteria.description: Exact character width measured via NSFont.advancementForGlyph()
Results cached per font family + size combination
Works correctly for all supported monospace fonts

steps.title: Implement WindowSizer with dynamic size calculation
steps.description: Create WindowSizer that computes the default NSRect for new windows. Formula: width = (charWidth * 110) + contentPadding + windowChrome. Height = width * 0.75, capped at 85% of target screen height. Apply minimum (60 chars wide, 400pt tall) and maximum (90% screen width) constraints. The target screen is the one containing the mouse cursor (NSEvent.mouseLocation -> NSScreen.screens.first(where: frame.contains)). Center the window rect on the target screen. Recalculate when theme changes (ThemeManager notification).

acceptance_criteria.description: Window width fits 110 characters of the current theme font
Height is 75% of width, capped at 85% screen height
Window centered on screen containing mouse cursor

steps.title: Set window minimum size constraints
steps.description: Configure NSWindow.minSize based on the minimum character count (60 chars) for the current font. This prevents users from resizing the window so small that markdown tables break. Also set contentMinSize on the window. The minimum should update if the theme font changes. Maximum size is not constrained (user can go full screen) — only the default size respects the 90% maximum.

acceptance_criteria.description: NSWindow.minSize set to 60 characters width + 400pt height
Minimum size updates when theme font changes
Full screen and system window management still work

architecture.system_compat: Full screen, split view, Stage Manager unaffected by minSize
